text.title: Design Tokens Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Design Tokens application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Tokens are organised in three tiers: primitive (raw value), semantic (purpose-named alias), component (usage-specific). Components never reference primitives directly.

rationale.source: Design Tokens W3C draft + Style Dictionary docs

rule.rationale: Skipping the semantic tier means rebranding requires touching every component; with semantics, you swap aliases.

rule.statement: Token values live in one canonical JSON file per tier; every platform output (CSS, JS, Swift, XML) is generated from those files. No hand-authored platform-specific tokens.
rule.rationale: Multiple sources guarantee drift; CSS vars and Swift constants will silently diverge.

rule.statement: Component code references semantic tokens (text/primary, surface/raised), never hex values or primitive token names.
rule.rationale: Hex values in component code defeat theming; primitive references couple components to specific palettes.

rule.statement: Token emission runs in CI on every PR that touches tokens. Outputs are committed (or published) deterministically.
rule.rationale: Local-only builds drift between developers; CI emission is the single auditable artefact.

rule.statement: A CI job compares token JSON to Figma variables (via Figma API or exported file). PRs that diverge fail unless an ADR documents the divergence.
rule.rationale: Without parity check, design and code paint themselves into different corners; the parity gate keeps both honest.

rule.statement: Spacing, typography, radii, and shadows must reference tokens; hardcoded pixel values are flagged by lint rules in component code.
rule.rationale: Hardcoded values bypass the system and accumulate; lint enforcement keeps the boundary clean.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
